km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស
km.Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandi: ណូរវីល, សេiនែម៉ារីtiមែ, ហាuteណូរម៉ានឌិe, ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandi: 法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利諾維爾
zh.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court於1195年出生。他於1220年之前與Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1262年在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利諾維爾享年67歲。

zh-hans.Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandi: 法国豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利諾維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court于1195年出生。他于1220年之前与Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1262年在法国豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利諾維爾享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Court
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

variants.name: Courtade
meanings: French (mainly southern): topographic name derived from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ), or a habitational name from (La) Courtade, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.

variants.name: Cournoyer
meanings: French Canadian: topographic name for someone living at a farm with a walnut tree in the farmyard, from French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) + noyer ‘walnut tree’. Compare Courneya .

History: This surname was originally a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne Pierre Hus dit Cournoyer from QC (a son of Paul Hus from Montigny in Seine-Maritime, France; see Hus ), who married Jeanne Vanet in QC in 1700. His descendants also bear altered forms of the former secondary surname, such as Courneya, and also the surname Paulette .

variants.name: Curt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Frieda.
English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Court  .
Americanized form of German   Kurt  .

variants.name: Curtis
meanings: English: nickname for a refined person, sometimes perhaps given ironically, from Middle English, Old French courteis, courtois, curtis ‘courtly, refined, urbane’ (derivative of Old French court; see Court 1).

variants.name: Lecourt
meanings: French: nickname for a physically small man (or ironically for a large man), a variant of Court , with fused masculine definite article le.

variants.name: Courtemanche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Amedee, Andre, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Lucien, Marcel, Normand, Ovide, Raoul.

French: habitational name from Courtemanche or Courdemanche, the name of several places in Somme, Sarthe, Eure, and Orne, derived from Latin cortis dominica ‘lord's farm or estate’ (see Court ). Compare Shortsleeve .
History: Antoine Courtemanche dit Jolicoeur from Bannes in Sarthe, France, married Élisabeth Haquin in Montreal, QC, in 1663.

variants.name: Courtier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Patrice.
French: habitational name from any of the places called Courtier (Seine-et-Marne, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence) or Courtière (Loir-et-Cher).
French: occupational name or perhaps a nickname from Old French courtier ‘arbitrator, judge’.

variants.name: Cort
meanings: Catalan: from cort ‘court’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’), an occupational name for someone who worked in a manorial court or a topographic name for someone who lived in or by one.
English: variant of   Court  .
Americanized form of Dutch, German, and Jewish   Kort  .

variants.name: Courchesne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Aime, Andre, Aurel, Emile, Gaston, Germain, Gilles, Jean-Claude, Marcel, Marcelle.

French Canadian: topographic name for someone living at a farm with an oak tree in the farmyard, from (Old) French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) + Old French chesne (French chêne) ‘oak’. Compare Courchaine .

variants.name: Courts
meanings: English: variant of Court , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Americanized form of German Kurtz or its Dutch cognate Koerts.

nameMeanings.name: Dampre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Pasquale, Dante, Donato, Enrico, Luigi, Natale, Nunzio, Alfonse, Antonino.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Amore, meaning ‘love’. D'Amore sometimes denoted a foundling or an illegitimate son, a ‘love child’.

en.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court was born in 1195. He married Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt before 1220, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1262, in Norville, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court est né en 1195. Il a épousé Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt avant 1220, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1262, à Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court wurde 1195 geboren. Er hat vor 1220 in Europa Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1262 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court è nato il nel 1195. Lui sposato Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt prima del 1220, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1262, nel Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandi: フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメノーヴィル
ja.lifeSummary: Dampre De Courtは1195年に生まれた。1220年以前にヨーロッパでMrs Sigr Dampre DeCourtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1262年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメノーヴィルで67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court(은)는 1195년 태어났다. 그는 1220년 이전 우럽에서 Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1262년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 노르빌에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court nasceu em 1195. Ele casou-se com Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt antes 1220, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1262, em Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court родился в 1195 г. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt перед 1220 г. в местности Европа. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1262 г. в местности норвилл, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Dampre De Court nació en 1195. Se casó con Mrs Sigr Dampre DeCourt antes de 1220, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1262, en Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 67 años.

th.–Female: ถึงแก่กรรม
th.Norville, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandi: เนอรวิลล์, สไอเน-แมริตอิเม, เฮาตเอ-เนอรแมนดี, ฝรั่งเศส
